metadata.name: Broken Link Crawler
metadata.description: Standalone Python crawler for one website that walks same-host pages, checks discovered links and assets, and prints a stdout summary.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This template renders a standalone `main.py` script plus `requirements.txt` for lightweight broken-link crawling on one website.

## What It Does
- Reads one `website_url` value as the crawl entrypoint.
- Crawls only the exact same host as that URL.
- Extracts anchor, image, stylesheet, and script URLs from HTML pages.
- Checks discovered same-host links and assets and records broken or unreachable targets with their source pages.
- Prints a plain stdout summary at the end of the run.

## Prerequisites
- Install the Python packages from `requirements.txt` in the runtime environment.
- Start with a small page limit on larger sites to avoid unintentional load.
- If the site needs authentication, add the appropriate request headers before running.

## Run
1. Render `main.py` and `requirements.txt`.
2. Install dependencies with `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
3. Review `website_url`, max pages, and request settings in the rendered file.
4. Run `python3 main.py` directly or from cron/systemd.

## Notes
- The crawler only follows and checks URLs on the exact same host as `website_url`.
- Use the start URL that matches the canonical host you want to audit, for example `https://www.example.com` versus `https://example.com`.
- Broken links are reported in stdout, but the script exits successfully unless the script itself encounters an execution error.

## References
- [Requests](https://requests.readthedocs.io/)
- [Beautiful Soup](https://www.crummy.com/software/BeautifulSoup/)

items.name: website_url
items.title: Website URL
items.description: Single crawl entrypoint. The crawler stays on this exact host.

items.name: max_pages
items.title: Max Pages
items.description: Maximum number of HTML pages to crawl.

items.name: request_timeout_seconds
items.title: Timeout Seconds
items.description: Timeout for each outbound HTTP request.

items.name: verify_tls
items.title: Verify TLS
items.description: Validate remote TLS certificates during requests.

items.name: request_headers_json
items.title: Request Headers JSON
items.description: JSON object of headers added to every request.

items.name: user_agent
items.title: User Agent
items.description: User-Agent header value used by the crawler.
